reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots in Tohoku but tool was called for Akita with no sakura filter. Response returns general Akita landmarks (Oyu Stone Circles, Lake Tazawa, Kubota Castle) none of which are primarily sakura spots. Sakura-famous Kakunodate (角館) is absent. Schema is solid with heritage_designations and multilingual labels. Mismatch between query intent and results is the core failure.
missing_entities: 角館 (Kakunodate sakura), 弘前公園 (Hirosaki Park), 北上展勝地
improvement_hint: Add kind/tag 'sakura' or 'cherry_blossom' to relevant spots so sakura-intent queries can filter; Kakunodate is a top Tohoku sakura spot and must appear in Akita results.

reasoning: Query asks for sake breweries in Niigata; tool returned MAFF GI food items (edamame, carrots, lotus root) — zero sake breweries. Niigata sake (越後杜氏, 久保田, 八海山, 越乃寒梅) is entirely absent. The data layer has no brewery data and the GI specialty tool cannot serve this query. A complete domain mismatch.
missing_entities: 八海山, 久保田, 越乃寒梅, 上善如水, 雪中梅
improvement_hint: Add sake brewery entities to the data layer (Wikidata Q items for major Niigata kura); expose a get_breweries or extend get_local_specialty with 'sake' category so the agent can retrieve them.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts with hot springs near Hakuba. Response includes municipal scrape items that are navigation/admin pages (観光課, 観光商工係, スポット紹介) rather than actual ski resorts or onsen. Hakuba47, Hakuba Goryu, Hakuba Happo-One are absent. Some Wikidata spots (Hakuba Oike lake, art museum, botanical garden) are legitimate entities but not ski resorts. Low semantic relevance; admin pages pollute results.
missing_entities: 白馬八方尾根スキー場, 白馬47, 白馬五竜スキー場, 白馬温泉
improvement_hint: Filter out municipal admin/navigation pages from scrape results; add ski resort entity type (P31=ski resort) and onsen kind to Hakuba entities so that ski+onsen co-queries can find them.

reasoning: Query asks best autumn foliage spots in Kansai; tool queried Kyoto only (missing Osaka, Nara, Hyogo). Response has high-quality Kyoto UNESCO WHS spots (Nijo Castle, Toji, Nishi-Honganji, Ninna-ji, Saiho-ji) with heritage_designations and multi-language labels. Iconic foliage spots like Eikan-do, Tofuku-ji, Arashiyama are absent. No foliage-specific metadata tag, but heritage data is excellent. Partial coverage due to single-prefecture scope.
missing_entities: 永観堂, 東福寺, 嵐山, 大阪城公園 (foliage)
improvement_hint: Add 'koyo' or 'autumn_foliage' kind/tag to spots; expand Kansai foliage query to cover multiple prefectures (Osaka, Nara, Hyogo) rather than Kyoto only.

reasoning: Query asks famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai; tool queried Nara. Response has relevant Nara spots (Kasuga Taisha, temples) via municipal scrape, correctly geolocated. However, items all named '神社・仏閣' (generic category label) rather than actual spot names — this is a schema pollution issue. Top Kansai sakura spots (Yoshino, Maruyama Park Kyoto, Himeji) absent. No heritage_designations on scrape items, no prominence_score.
missing_entities: 吉野山 (Yoshino sakura), 円山公園 (Maruyama Kyoto), 姫路城 (himeji)
improvement_hint: Fix municipal scrape name extraction — use specific spot name not category label '神社・仏閣'. Add sakura kind tag. Expand to multi-prefecture for Kansai queries. Expose prominence_score on scrape items.

reasoning: Query asks for stargazing spots on Okinawa remote islands. Response shows Okinawa mainland UNESCO-heritage castles (Shuri, Nakagusuku, Nakijin, Tamaudun) — correct prefecture but no remote island entities. Iriomote, Ishigaki, Miyako, Kerama islands are absent. No dark-sky or stargazing metadata. Kumejima appears briefly but truncated. Heritage designations are well-structured.
missing_entities: 石垣島, 宮古島, 西表島, 慶良間諸島, 久米島 (stargazing spots)
improvement_hint: Add island-level entities for major Ryukyu islands with kinds including 'island', 'stargazing'; expose safety_keywords_detected for dark-sky context; filter/expand by island municipality for remote island queries.

reasoning: Query asks for rural Hokkaido onsen ryokan. Response is dominated by urban Sapporo chain hotels (ANA Holiday Inn, ANA Crowne Plaza) and generic apartments in Niseko, none tagged as onsen_ryokan except 'ビジネスホテル 串本温泉' which is actually in Wakayama (wrong prefecture). Rural onsen like Noboribetsu, Jozankei, Sounkyo, Tokachigawa are absent. lodging_type field exists but not used for onsen filtering.
missing_entities: 登別温泉, 定山渓温泉, 層雲峡温泉, 十勝川温泉, 川湯温泉
wrong_facts: ビジネスホテル 串本温泉 is in Kushimoto Wakayama not Hokkaido (likely a data error in prefecture_code)
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=onsen_ryokan filtering capability and ingest major Hokkaido onsen ryokan from Wikidata/OSM; fix prefecture attribution for Kushimoto hotel appearing in Hokkaido results.

reasoning: Query asks for islands in the Seto Inland Sea. Tool queried Kagawa and returned mainland attractions (Ritsurin Garden, Marugame Castle, Kotohira). Naoshima (直島), Teshima (豊島), Shodoshima (小豆島) — the canonical Seto Island answers — are absent. Response is accurate for Kagawa but semantically misses the islands. The heritage data on returned items is high quality.
missing_entities: 直島 (Naoshima), 豊島 (Teshima), 小豆島 (Shodoshima), 女木島, 男木島
improvement_hint: Add island entities to Kagawa data with kinds='island'; add Setouchi region grouping so multi-island queries work; Naoshima (art island) is a globally known entity that must be indexed.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima; tool returned METI craft specialties. Response includes 阿波正藍しじら織 (Awa Hon-ai Shijira Weave) and 阿波和紙, both directly related to indigo/traditional crafts. Schema excellent: METI keys, source_url, bilingual descriptions, designation dates, production_area. Missing: workshop/experiential venues for hands-on dyeing. Only 3 items returned; actual 藍染 experience spots from DMO would strengthen answer.
missing_entities: 藍染体験施設 (specific workshops), 徳島市藍染工房
improvement_hint: Supplement METI designations with DMO scrape results for hands-on workshop entities (体験施設); add kinds='workshop' or 'craft_experience' to enable experiential queries.

reasoning: Query asks for shukubo (temple lodging) on Koyasan. Tool queried all Wakayama hotels returning generic business hotels and HOTEL KII TANABE — no shukubo at all. Koyasan's famous shukubo like Ekoin, Fukuchiin, Rengejo-in are entirely absent. lodging_type=shukubo is missing from schema. Critical domain mismatch between query expectation and result.
missing_entities: 恵光院 (Ekoin), 福智院 (Fukuchiin), 蓮華定院, 高野山全般の宿坊
improvement_hint: Add shukubo as a lodging_type and index Koyasan's ~50 shukubo from OSM/Wikidata; municipality filter for 高野町 should surface these; critical gap for one of Japan's top cultural lodging experiences.

reasoning: Query asks for samurai districts outside Kyoto. search_area returned 82 matches with good diversity: Chiran (知覧武家屋敷 prominently, outside Kyoto, heritage designated), Aizu (会津武家屋敷), Shimane Buke Yashiki, and Kanazawa Nagamachi description via scrape. Kanazawa's Nagamachi is in a scrape blob (good content but low-structure). Hagi (萩) samurai district is absent. Prominence ranking puts Chiran second which is reasonable.
missing_entities: 萩 (Hagi) 武家屋敷, 弘前 武家屋敷, 飫肥 武家屋敷
improvement_hint: Add Hagi and Obi samurai districts as distinct Wikidata entities; upgrade Nagamachi from scrape blob to structured spot with kinds=['samurai_district','historic_district'] and heritage_designation (重要伝統的建造物群保存地区).

reasoning: Query asks for summer festivals in Akita in Indonesian. Response returns 3 items via bunka_intangible plus national_heritage section with UNESCO items. Critically: 秋田竿燈まつり (Kanto Festival) — one of Japan's three great summer festivals — is absent from items but the query is well-matched otherwise. Dainichido Bugaku and Kakunodate Festival are legitimate. UNESCO IDs, inscription years, bilingual + Indonesian translations, source_urls all present. Excellent metadata.
missing_entities: 秋田竿燈まつり (Akita Kanto Festival, UNESCO ICH)
improvement_hint: Akita Kanto Festival (竿燈まつり) is a UNESCO Intangible CH (2014 inscription as part of Tohoku festivals) and must appear; check bunka_intangible ingestion for this key festival.

reasoning: Query asks for small port towns in Sanin region. Tool queried Shimane. Response leads with Iwami Ginzan (UNESCO WHS, prominence 1.0) and Matsue Castle — strong on heritage landmarks. Izumo Taisha appears. However, actual fishing port towns (温泉津, 鞆の浦, 境港) are absent. The recall of known landmark (出雲大社) is present. Results are accurate Shimane spots but miss the fishing port intent.
missing_entities: 温泉津 (Yunotsu fishing village), 境港, 鞆の浦 (Tomo-no-ura)
improvement_hint: Add kinds='fishing_port' and 'port_town' to relevant entities; 温泉津 is a UNESCO WHS component and must be in Shimane data; expand Sanin query to cover Tottori for Tomo-no-ura.

reasoning: Query asks for washi paper making experiences in Gifu. Response returns 8 METI craft items including Mino Washi (美濃和紙) and Hida Shunkei. 美濃和紙 is present (key craft for Gifu washi). Schema excellent with source_url, bilingual descriptions, craft_id, designation_date. Missing: experiential workshop venues and hands-on making spots. 美濃和紙あかりアート館 or Warabi Paper Museum not indexed.
missing_entities: 美濃和紙の里会館 (workshop venue), 和紙体験施設
improvement_hint: Add kinds='workshop' and link to DMO experience pages for Mino Washi making; the METI designation is confirmed but agents need venue-level data to answer 'where can I experience' queries.

reasoning: Query asks for fishing villages on Noto Peninsula. Tool queried Ishikawa. Response leads with Kanazawa Castle and Kenroku-en — urban Kanazawa, not Noto. Noto-specific fishing villages (輪島, 珠洲, 能登町 fishing ports, 白米千枚田) are absent. Seven-o-castle (七尾城) appears which is in Noto but is a castle not a fishing village. The municipality filter was not used for Noto specifically.
missing_entities: 輪島 (Wajima), 珠洲 (Suzu), 白米千枚田, 能登半島 漁村
improvement_hint: Add municipality='能登町' or region filter; add kinds='fishing_village' and 'port_town' to Noto entities; 輪島 and 白米千枚田 are internationally known Noto landmarks that must appear in Ishikawa results.

reasoning: Query asks for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. Response has 80 matches but is dominated by Tottori Sakyu results (multiple Tottori tourism pages, GI product 鳥取砂丘らっきょう). 高砂貝塚 (Hokkaido) is a midden/shell mound, not a sand dune. Lesser-known dunes like 中田島砂丘 (Hamamatsu), 吹上浜 (Kagoshima), 砂の美術館 are absent. No dune-specific kind/type tag. Municipal scrape results lack coordinates on some items.
missing_entities: 中田島砂丘 (Hamamatsu), 吹上浜 (Kagoshima), 砂の美術館 (Tottori, but iconic)
wrong_facts: 高砂貝塚 is a shell midden not a sand dune — misleading placement in results
improvement_hint: Add kinds='sand_dune' to all dune entities; index 中田島砂丘 (Shizuoka) and 吹上浜 (Kagoshima) as distinct Wikidata spots; suppress irrelevant midden matches for dune queries via kind filtering.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional crafts in Sanin (山陰) region in Chinese. Tool queried Shimane crafts in zh. Response returns 4 METI items: Iwami-yaki (石見焼), Sekishu Washi (石州和紙), Unshū Soroban (雲州そろばん), Izumo Stone Lanterns (出云石灯笼) — all legitimate Shimane/Sanin crafts with accurate Chinese translations, source_urls, designation_dates. Tottori crafts (因州和紙 etc.) missing as only Shimane queried, but core answer is strong.
missing_entities: 因州和紙 (Tottori), 弓浜絣 (Tottori)
improvement_hint: Expand Sanin query to include Tottori when region='山陰' is implied; add a region grouping endpoint or accept region=sanin parameter to cover both prefectures.

reasoning: Query asks for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool queried Hokkaido broadly. Response returns top-prominence spots (Matsumae Castle, Goryokaku, Showa-shinzan, Sapporo Clock Tower) — none are lavender fields. Farm Tomita (富良野) is not present, and no lesser-known lavender fields appear. No 'flower_field' or 'lavender' kind/tag in schema. Query intent completely unmatched.
missing_entities: ファーム富田 (Farm Tomita), かみふらのラベンダー, 彩香の里, 北星山ラベンダー園
improvement_hint: Add kinds='flower_field' and 'lavender' to agricultural/tourist farm entities; index Hokkaido lavender farms (Farm Tomita and others) with Wikidata or DMO scrape; add seasonal metadata for bloom periods.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional towns in Kyushu. Tool queried Oita returning Usa Jingu, Beppu Hells, Oka Castle. These are legitimate Oita landmarks but not 'traditional towns' (古い町並み). Query was in Tagalog. Usuki (臼杵) stone Buddhas appear partially. Traditional townscapes like Yanagawa, Arita, Hita, Saiki are absent. Beppu Tower and tourist sites dominate over historic streetscapes.
missing_entities: 日田 (Hita historical town), 杵築武家屋敷, 豊後竹田 (Taketa historical town)
improvement_hint: Add kinds='historic_town' and 'traditional_townscape' to entities; Hita (日田) and Usuki (臼杵) are classic Oita historic towns that should rank highly for townscape queries; add IPDT designation tags.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional old farmhouses (古民家) to stay in Hokuriku. Tool queried Ishikawa hotels. Response leads with 'Student housing 1' (null name), AB Hotel generic, ANA Crowne Plaza — no kominka (古民家) lodging. No lodging_type=kominka in schema. Noto Peninsula kominka stays, Shirakawa-go gassho farmhouses (Gifu but near), and Kanazawa machiya lodging are entirely absent. Complete domain mismatch.
missing_entities: 白川郷 合掌造り民宿, 金沢 町家ステイ, 能登 古民家宿
improvement_hint: Add lodging_type=kominka and machiya; ingest Shirakawa-go gassho-style minshuku; Noto earthquake recovery kominka stays are a topical need; filter hotels by traditional style keywords from OSM tags.

reasoning: Query asks for mountain shrines on Kii Peninsula. Tool called get_japan_heritage for Wakayama. Response includes Japan Heritage stories: Wakanoura (scenic beauty), Saigoku Pilgrimage — relevant cultural context but not specifically mountain shrines. Kumano Hongu, Nachi Taisha, Koya-san are absent in truncated preview. Heritage authority (Agency for Cultural Affairs), source URLs, bilingual body text all excellent. Truncation may hide more relevant items.
missing_entities: 熊野本宮大社, 熊野速玉大社, 那智大社, 丹生都比売神社 (UNESCO WHS shrines)
improvement_hint: Kumano Kodo UNESCO WHS shrine components must be top-ranked for mountain shrine queries in Wakayama; add kinds='mountain_shrine' and ensure Kumano Taisha entities surface before scenic beauty stories.

reasoning: Query asks for summer fireworks festivals along Sea of Japan coast. Tool called get_festivals for Niigata with 129 results. 長岡まつり大花火大会 (Nagaoka Grand Fireworks) appears in the visible results — one of Japan's top three fireworks festivals, directly relevant. Items include scraped schema events with dates (2026-08-02/03 for Nagaoka). First two results (moss phlox, lily festival) are not fireworks events but Nagaoka appears third. Lack of event_type=fireworks filter means off-topic events appear first.
improvement_hint: Add event_type='hanabi' or kinds='fireworks_festival' to enable fireworks-intent filtering; the relevant event (Nagaoka) is present but buried; add Sea of Japan coast tag to cross-prefecture fireworks queries.

reasoning: Query asks for traditional bamboo crafts in Oita. Response returns 2 METI items: Beppu Bamboo Crafts (別府竹細工) — directly on point — and Hakata Ori (shared with Fukuoka/Oita). 別府竹細工 is the canonical answer, correctly ranked, with full bilingual description, METI craft_id, production_area, source_url. Schema is exemplary. Only 2 items in Oita craft which reflects the actual METI designation count. Slight improvement: add Wikidata qid for 別府竹細工.
improvement_hint: Link METI craft records to Wikidata qid for cross-referencing; add kinds='bamboo_craft' as a tag for craft-type filtering; expose wikidata_qid on specialty items for agent traceability.

reasoning: Query asks for cycling routes in rural Shikoku. Tool queried Ehime spots returning castles and heritage buildings (Uwajima Castle, Matsuyama Castle, Ozu Castle, Uchiko-za). No cycling route entities, no Shimanami Kaido (しまなみ海道) — the world-famous cycling route between Honshu and Shikoku through Ehime — appears. The route is arguably the single most famous cycling destination in Japan. Heritage data on returned items is good but query intent is unmet.
missing_entities: しまなみ海道 (Shimanami Kaido cycling route), 佐田岬半島, サイクリングロード
improvement_hint: Index Shimanami Kaido as a distinct spot/route entity with kinds='cycling_route'; add activity metadata (sports, outdoor) to enable activity-intent queries; Shimanami Kaido must be in Ehime top results.

reasoning: Query asks where to see kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. Tool called get_traditional_arts nationally. Response leads with Bunraku (UNESCO ICH) then Kabuki (UNESCO ICH, Q199701) — kabuki is present and correctly identified. 183 total items. The response provides the art form data but no venue-level information (specific rural kabuki theaters like 金丸座 in Kotohira, 旧金毘羅大芝居). UNESCO IDs, qids, inscription years, bilingual metadata all excellent. Venue gap is the main weakness.
missing_entities: 金丸座/旧金毘羅大芝居 (Kotohira, oldest kabuki theater), 地歌舞伎 (village kabuki venues)
improvement_hint: Add kabuki venue entities with kinds='kabuki_theater'; link 旧金毘羅大芝居 (Kotohira) and regional 地歌舞伎 stages as spots; agents need venue location data not just art-form taxonomy to answer 'where to see' queries.
